## User Request In src/shared/workflow/workflow.js, the extractTasks function uses a strict regex (/\|\s*(\d+)\s*\|\s*(\w[\w-]*)\s*\|.../) to parse the ### Tasks table. While you recently added the structured tasks array to the plan_written tool schema (which is the ideal approach), falling back to this regex is risky. LLMs frequently hallucinate minor Markdown table formatting (e.g., dropping a pipe, adding extra whitespace). Consider using a relaxed Markdown parser for this fallback, or strictly enforcing the plan_written tool validation to ensure the JSON array is always present. ## Triage Report - Classification: FEATURE - Complexity: LOW - Summary: Improve robustness of task extraction by enforcing structured data in the 'plan_written' tool and relaxing the fallback regex parser. Context learned: README.md lines 106-109 already describe RTK user-facing behavior: optional, fail-open, rewrites eligible agent-initiated shell commands, missing warning, manual !/!! not rewritten, exclusions in RTK config. docs/index.md and docs/quickstart.md mention RTK as optional/fail-open. docs/contributing.md lines 47-50 says RTK is optional at runtime but repo Deno tasks use it, matching deno.json tasks where ci/test/check invoke rtk.
